meaning: n.精神抖擞者,有力的反驳
examples: She opened the speech with a real zinger.
I love listening to my friends talk and laugh, throwing in a zinger of my own now and again.
The panelists are left to compress their inquiries into one good zinger of a question.
In this debate, it was just another major issue winnowed down to zinger-ready bite size.
My brother had never been the most rational of agents, but this one was the ill zinger.
